Feedback? Ideas? Complaints? Suggestions? Request for report about other area? send me a message!
This page was generated using data obtained on various dates including 2023-09-29, 2023-10-02, 2023-10-16, 2023-10-15, -data-was-not-downloaded-for-now-, 2023-10-13. Please, send me a message if you want it updated!




Remember to check whether edits make sense! All reports are on this page because these tasks require human judgment to verify whether the proposed edit makes sense. Some proposed edits will be wrong, in such case they should be not done and problem reported

wikipedia tag links to 404
Wikipedia article linked from OSM object using wikipedia tag is missing.
Often article was moved without leaving redirect and wikipedia tag should be edited to point to the new one.
Article may be deleted and no longer existing, or link was never valid. In such cases wikipedia tag should be deleted.

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking from wikipedia and wikidata tag to a violation of law
linked wikidata entry (Q456343) is about a violation of law, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata

linked wikidata entry (Q165145) is about an animal or plant (and not an individual one), so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present

subject:wikipedia=* or model:wikipedia tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present

linked wikidata entry (Q1333739) is about a social issue, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking from wikipedia and wikidata tag to a public transport network
linked wikidata entry (Q1963809) is about a public transport network, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* or operator:wikipedia tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking from wikipedia tag to an animal or plant (and not an individual one)
linked wikidata entry (Q1555379) is about an animal or plant (and not an individual one), so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking from wikipedia tag to a human
linked wikidata entry (Q1503560) is about a human, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* or name:wikipedia tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking from wikipedia tag to a public transport network
linked wikidata entry (Q12645) is about a public transport network, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* or operator:wikipedia tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata

wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not
wikidata and wikipedia tags link to a different objects, because wikidata tag points to a redirect that should be followed
article claims to point to disambig, wikidata does not. wikidata tag is likely to be correct, wikipedia tag almost certainly is not

wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for brand prefixed tags
brand:wikidata and brand:wikipedia tags link to a different objects, because brand:wikidata tag points to a redirect that should be followed
article claims to point to disambig, brand:wikidata does not. brand:wikidata tag is likely to be correct, brand:wikipedia tag almost certainly is not
an affected OSM element that may be improved

wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for subject prefixed tags
subject:wikidata and subject:wikipedia tags link to a different objects, because subject:wikidata tag points to a redirect that should be followed
article claims to point to disambig, subject:wikidata does not. subject:wikidata tag is likely to be correct, subject:wikipedia tag almost certainly is not

wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for name:etymology prefixed tags
name:etymology:wikidata and name:etymology:wikipedia tags link to a different objects, because name:etymology:wikidata tag points to a redirect that should be followed

wikipedia wikidata mismatch - for architect prefixed tags
architect:wikidata and architect:wikipedia tags link to a different objects (Q1571656 vs (missing) wikidata id assigned to linked Wikipedia article)
